articles.title: AirPods, Touch Bars, and the rest of Tim Cook’s legacy
articles.summary: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO, with John Ternus as his likely successor. This leadership change could bring significant strategic shifts at the influential tech company.

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman, claiming fraud. A trial begins April 27th in Oakland, marking a messy legal battle between the cofounders over the company's direction and leadership.

articles.title: China’s DeepSeek previews new AI model a year after jolting US rivals
articles.summary: Chinese AI company DeepSeek previewed its V4 model, claiming it matches leading US competitors like OpenAI and Google. The open-source model shows major improvements in coding capabilities.

articles.title: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins stacked StrictlyVC SF lineup for April 30 event
articles.summary: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins StrictlyVC San Francisco on April 30 to discuss operating at scale in the AI age.

articles.title: Nothing introduces an AI-powered dictation tool
articles.summary: Nothing launches an AI-powered dictation tool supporting over 100 languages for on-device use.

articles.title: DeepSeek previews new AI model that ‘closes the gap’ with frontier models
articles.summary: DeepSeek unveiled new AI models with architectural improvements that are more efficient and performant than V3.2, nearly matching frontier models on reasoning benchmarks.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The funding capitalizes on demand for alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality as a free, open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative $5K marketing stunt attracted thousands of engineers competing to solve puzzles.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware claims to use quantum-safe ML-KEM encryption, making it the first confirmed ransomware family using post-quantum cryptography standards from NIST.

articles.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency patch for ASP.NET Core vulnerability CVE-2026-40372 affecting Linux and macOS. The high-severity flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges through cryptographic signature verification bypass.

articles.title: Contrary to popular superstition, AES 128 is just fine in a post-quantum world
articles.summary: Cryptography engineer Filippo Valsorda clarifies that AES-128 remains secure in a post-quantum world, contrary to popular misconception. The encryption standard's 2^128 possible keys make brute-force attacks impractical.

articles.title: The Download: supercharged scams and studying AI healthcare
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are leveraging generative AI tools like ChatGPT to conduct sophisticated scams, including phishing, deepfakes, and vulnerability scans at unprecedented scale and speed.

articles.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
articles.summary: AI tools are increasingly used in hospitals for note-taking, patient record analysis, and medical imaging interpretation. However, despite showing accuracy, evidence that these tools actually improve patient health outcomes remains unclear.

articles.title: The Download: introducing the Nature issue
articles.summary: Human influence now permeates every corner of Earth, from microplastics in rainforests to artificial light in oceans. The Nature issue explores what nature means today and whether technology can restore natural environments.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 introduces a million-token context window enabling AI agents to process vast amounts of information effectively. This advancement significantly expands what agents can accomplish with long-form understanding.

articles.title: How to Use Transformers.js in a Chrome Extension
articles.summary: Guide on integrating Transformers.js library into Chrome extensions for running AI models locally. Enables developers to add machine learning capabilities directly in browser extensions.

articles.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
articles.summary: Gemma 4 VLA (Vision Language Model) demonstrated running on Jetson Orin Nano Super, showcasing efficient edge AI deployment capabilities for multimodal tasks.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Microsoft's Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development, exploring emerging trends and technological advancements that will shape the industry.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and handling. These AI solutions enable companies to work more efficiently while expanding their imaginative capabilities.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, enabling developers to build AI-powered agricultural solutions. The toolkit aims to modernize farming through advanced technology and sustainability.

articles.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
articles.summary: Google's TPUs are specialized processors designed to handle increasingly demanding AI workloads efficiently. A new video explains how these tensor processing units power modern artificial intelligence applications.

articles.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
articles.summary: Google announces its first Austrian data center in Kronstorf, creating 100 direct jobs and expanding its European infrastructure presence in the Alps region.

articles.title: We're launching two specialized TPUs for the agentic era.
articles.summary: Google launches eighth-generation TPUs with two specialized chips designed for the agentic era of AI, enabling more advanced autonomous AI systems.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new method for resilient distributed AI training that can withstand failures. This approach decouples communication and computation for improved efficiency in large-scale model training.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technology and accelerate their digital transformation initiatives.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces granular audio tags enabling precise control for expressive AI speech generation. This next-generation audio model offers enhanced customization for natural-sounding voice synthesis.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics with real-time photorealism improvements. OpenAI develops a superapp while MiniMax releases M2.7 model.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: How AI models use real-time cryptocurrency data to interpret market behaviour
articles.summary: AI models leverage real-time cryptocurrency data streams to interpret market behavior, with crypto's volatile and unpredictable patterns providing valuable training complexity compared to static traditional datasets.

articles.title: The billion-dollar startup with a different idea for AI
articles.summary: Yann LeCun's newly funded AMI Labs pursues alternative AI development beyond large language models, focusing on modular components for specific use-cases rather than general-purpose systems.

articles.title: NVIDIA and Google infrastructure cuts AI inference costs
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Google unveiled A5X bare-metal instances designed to slash AI inference costs by up to 10x per token while boosting throughput, utilizing advanced hardware and networking technology to scale efficiently across thousands of GPUs.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning in complex indoor environments, overcoming traditional system limitations.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, creating significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a critical tradeoff between efficiency and generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems around them represent the true competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that the harness engineering, not the model itself, is becoming the key moat.

articles.title: Google DeepMind Introduces Decoupled DiLoCo: An Asynchronous Training Architecture Achieving 88% Goodput Under High Hardware Failure Rates
articles.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Decoupled DiLoCo, an asynchronous training architecture that decouples compute into fault-isolated islands, enabling large language model training across distant data centers without tight synchronization, achieving 88% goodput under high hardware failure rates.

articles.title: Mend Releases AI Security Governance Framework: Covering Asset Inventory, Risk Tiering, AI Supply Chain Security, and Maturity Model
articles.summary: Mend released an AI Security Governance Framework addressing the gap between rapid AI adoption and slow security oversight in organizations. The framework covers asset inventory, risk tiering, supply chain security, and maturity models for teams without existing AI security programs.

articles.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5, a Fully Retrained Agentic Model That Scores 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 and 84.9% on GDPval
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.5, a fully retrained agentic model that completes multi-step tasks autonomously with minimal human direction, achieving 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 and 84.9% on GDPval benchmarks.

articles.title: The Microsoft Surface Pro is nearly 40% off at Best Buy - and we highly recommend it
articles.summary: Microsoft Surface Pro 13-inch is discounted nearly 40% at Best Buy, saving customers $800 on this powerful 2-in-1 laptop device.

articles.title: I tried ChatGPT Images 2.0: A fun, huge leap - and surprisingly useful for real work
articles.summary: ChatGPT Images 2.0 delivers impressive image generation with practical work applications, though occasional errors occur. It effectively handles branding, text, and infographics.

articles.title: I put GPT-5.5 through a 10-round test: It scored 93/100, losing points only for exuberance
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 achieved 93/100 in testing, demonstrating powerful capabilities but occasionally ignoring simple instructions, revealing a trade-off between intelligence and controllability.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents is now accessible to everyone using local small language models, democratizing technology previously reserved for major tech companies.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular framework for serving machine learning models due to its lightweight, fast, and user-friendly design. It simplifies the process of training, deploying, and serving Scikit-learn models.

articles.title: AI Agent Memory Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Stateless AI agents lack memory of previous interactions, making each call independent. The article explains AI agent memory across three difficulty levels.

articles.title: I Built an AI Pipeline for Kindle Highlights
articles.summary: A developer created a local, free AI pipeline that automatically cleans, structures, and summarizes Kindle highlights for better reading management.

articles.title: How to Improve Claude Code Performance with Automated Testing
articles.summary: The article discusses using automated testing to enhance Claude Code performance and maximize its capabilities for developers.

articles.title: How to Select Variables Robustly in a Scoring Model
articles.summary: Selecting stable variables is more important than quantity for effective scoring models. The article explains how to identify robust variables for better model performance.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity improvements using the system.

articles.title: Tag, You’re It: GeForce NOW Levels Up Game Discovery With Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ Labels
articles.summary: GeForce NOW adds in-app labels for Xbox Game Pass and Ubisoft+ games, improving game discovery. The update helps gamers easily identify available titles and maximize their cloud gaming membership value.

articles.title: Making Sense of the Early Universe
articles.summary: Article discusses understanding the early universe's formation and development. Content details are unavailable in the provided text.

articles.title: Amazon Quick for marketing: From scattered data to strategic action
articles.summary: Amazon Quick solves marketing's data fragmentation problem by connecting scattered systems and tools into a unified platform. It enables marketers to quickly access insights and take action without manually moving data between systems.

articles.title: Applying multimodal biological foundation models across therapeutics and patient care
articles.summary: Multimodal biological foundation models integrate fragmented healthcare data like genomics, medical images, and EHR records to improve disease diagnosis, treatment prediction, and personalized medicine decisions.

articles.title: Cost-effective multilingual audio transcription at scale with Parakeet-TDT and AWS Batch
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Parakeet-TDT model deployed on AWS Batch enables cost-effective multilingual audio transcription at scale, using a Token-and-Duration Transducer architecture to achieve faster-than-real-time inference while reducing ASR costs.

articles.title: Federated Learning Without the Refactoring Overhead Using NVIDIA FLARE
articles.summary: NVIDIA FLARE enables federated learning without requiring code refactoring, allowing organizations to train models on sensitive data that cannot be moved, making FL practical for real-world applications.

articles.title: Winning a Kaggle Competition with Generative AI–Assisted Coding
articles.summary: Three LLM agents generated 600,000+ lines of code and ran 850 experiments to win first place in a Kaggle competition. AI-assisted coding with GPU acceleration enables rapid iteration and testing in modern ML competitions.

articles.title: Simplify Sparse Deep Learning with Universal Sparse Tensor in nvmath-python
articles.summary: NVIDIA introduces Universal Sparse Tensor (UST) in nvmath-python, decoupling tensor sparsity from memory layout for improved flexibility and performance in sparse deep learning applications.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030 and is developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint while making solutions publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences drive cutting-edge development in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for vision-based models.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand how these complex systems make decisions, advancing safer and more trustworthy AI.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder to map objects to images, which are then corrupted by noise. The approach quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.
